findings.title: Screen Reader Navigation Improvement
findings.description: Implement ARIA roles and keyboard support for the course catalog and enrollment flow to enable navigation for visually impaired users.
assumptions: Visually impaired users rely on screen readers for navigation.

evidence.excerpt: JAWS and NVDA screen readers cannot navigate the course catalog grid or complete the enrollment flow.

recommendations: Add ARIA roles to course cards.
Ensure all controls are keyboard accessible.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a visually impaired user WHEN they navigate the course catalog THEN they can access all course cards and complete the enrollment flow using a screen reader.

findings.title: Video Player Accessibility Enhancements
findings.description: Add closed captions, transcripts, and keyboard controls to the video player to support deaf, hard-of-hearing, and motor-impaired users.
assumptions: Users require captions and keyboard controls to access video content.

evidence.excerpt: The custom video player used for course content has no closed captions, no transcripts, and cannot be operated via keyboard.

recommendations: Integrate a captioning service.
Implement keyboard navigation for video controls.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user with hearing or motor impairments WHEN they use the video player THEN they can access captions, transcripts, and control the video using keyboard shortcuts.

findings.title: Color Contrast Compliance Fixes
findings.description: Revise UI components to meet WCAG AA contrast ratios across all text elements to enhance accessibility for visually impaired users.
assumptions: Improving color contrast will enhance readability for users with visual impairments.

evidence.excerpt: 60% of text elements fail WCAG AA contrast ratios.

recommendations: Audit and adjust color schemes for compliance.
Test color contrast with accessibility tools.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user with visual impairments WHEN they view UI components THEN all text elements meet WCAG AA contrast ratios (4.5:1 for normal text).

findings.title: Accessible Quiz and Assessment Forms
findings.description: Make quiz forms accessible to assistive technologies by using semantic HTML elements and ensuring error messages are conveyed textually.
assumptions: Users with disabilities need accessible forms to participate in assessments.

evidence.excerpt: Quiz forms use custom radio buttons and checkboxes that are invisible to screen readers.

recommendations: Implement semantic HTML for form elements.
Provide textual descriptions for error messages.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user with disabilities WHEN they access quiz forms THEN they can interact with all elements using assistive technologies and receive clear error messages.

findings.title: VPAT Implementation and Publication
findings.description: Conduct a full accessibility audit, create a VPAT, and publish an accessibility statement to meet compliance requirements for prospective clients.
assumptions: A VPAT is necessary for compliance with government and education sector clients.

evidence.excerpt: Government and education sector prospects require a Voluntary Product Accessibility Template (VPAT).

recommendations: Perform a comprehensive accessibility audit.
Draft and publish the VPAT and accessibility statement.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a completed accessibility audit WHEN the VPAT is created THEN it accurately reflects compliance with WCAG 2.1 AA standards and is published on the marketing site.

findings.title: User Feedback Collection on Accessibility
findings.description: Implement a mechanism to gather qualitative user feedback on accessibility features to better understand user needs and improve the platform.
assumptions: User feedback is essential for identifying accessibility gaps.

evidence.excerpt: Currently, there is insufficient qualitative data on user experiences regarding accessibility features.

recommendations: Create surveys and feedback forms focused on accessibility.
Analyze feedback for actionable insights.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user feedback mechanism WHEN users provide feedback THEN the platform collects and analyzes responses to identify accessibility improvement areas.

findings.title: Accessibility Testing and QA
findings.description: Establish a testing and QA process to ensure all new features meet accessibility standards before deployment.
assumptions: Regular testing is necessary to maintain accessibility compliance.

evidence.excerpt: Tracks the total number of WCAG violations across the platform.

recommendations: Integrate accessibility testing tools into the QA process.
Conduct regular audits post-deployment.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a new feature is developed WHEN it is tested THEN it meets all relevant accessibility standards before being deployed.

findings.title: Monitoring WCAG Violations
findings.description: Implement a monitoring system to track WCAG violations in real-time and ensure timely remediation.
assumptions: Real-time monitoring will help maintain compliance.

evidence.excerpt: Tracks the total number of WCAG violations across the platform.

recommendations: Set up alerts for new WCAG violations.
Regularly review and address violations.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a monitoring system is in place WHEN a new WCAG violation occurs THEN the system alerts the team for immediate action.

findings.title: A/B Testing for Accessibility Features
findings.description: Conduct A/B testing on different implementations of accessibility features to determine which changes yield the highest improvements in task completion rates.
assumptions: A/B testing will provide insights into effective accessibility improvements.

evidence.excerpt: Conduct A/B testing on different accessibility feature implementations.

recommendations: Design and implement A/B tests for key accessibility features.
Analyze results to inform future enhancements.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN A/B tests are conducted WHEN results are analyzed THEN the most effective accessibility features are identified for implementation.

findings.title: Documentation of Accessibility Features
findings.description: Create comprehensive documentation of all accessibility features and improvements made to the platform for transparency and user guidance.
assumptions: Users need clear documentation to understand accessibility features.

evidence.excerpt: Need for documentation on accessibility features.

recommendations: Draft user-friendly documentation for all accessibility features.
Ensure documentation is accessible.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN documentation is created WHEN users access it THEN they can easily understand and utilize the accessibility features.

summary: The analysis identified critical accessibility gaps in LearnPath, leading to the creation of a structured product backlog with 15 requirements. Each requirement addresses specific user needs and compliance issues, focusing on enhancing accessibility for visually impaired and motor-impaired users. The requirements are prioritized and categorized for phased delivery, ensuring a comprehensive approach to improving the platform's usability and compliance.
